summary: A Kalshi market and its settlement can share one ticker, so the local `resources` store must key on (resource_type, id) rather than id alone.
reason: Under the generator's id-only primary key, upserting a settlement clobbered the market row that shared its ticker (and vice versa), corrupting winrate/attribution/heatmap; the v1-to-v2 rebuild backfills `updated_at` before copying because SQLite resolves column names at prepare time and aborts the migration if a legacy table predates that column.
validated_outcome: internal/store/composite_pk_test.go covers fresh-DB composite PK, v1-to-v2 migration including a pre-updated_at legacy table, and idempotent re-open.
